Adding and Customizing Queries

Using XLAST, you can create queries that your title can send to the Xbox Live service. These queries update or obtain information from the tables associated with a competition's database. This section describes the default queries that XLAST creates for you. It also explains how to add a query and how to customize an existing query.

  • Default Queries
  • Adding Queries
  • Customizing Existing Queries

Default Queries

By default, XLAST generates a set of queries for single-elimination competitions that your title is likely to need. Each query has its own node in the project tree. The default queries are listed in the following table.

Query Name Attribute Sensitive Description
Available Competitions Yes Searches for competitions that the player can join.
Cometition Details Yes Obtains the details of a specific competition.
Competition Standings No Retrieves the current standings of all players in a competition.
Competitions I Created Yes Finds all competitions that the current player created.
My Competitions No Retrieves all competitions in which the current player is registered as a participant.
Topology Yes Searches the specified competition's topology. For more information, see XOnlineCompetitionTopologySingleElimination.
Update Title Attributes Yes Updates the specified custom attributes.

You can modify any of the default queries to suit your needs.

If you find that you do not need one or more of the default queries, you can delete the unneeded query or queries. Simply right-click a query you do not want and choose Remove.

Adding Queries

To add a new query to the project, use the following procedure.

  1. Right-click the Queries node in the project tree and select Add New Query.
  2. In the dialog box that appears, set the name of the query. Names can contain alphanumeric characters and spaces.
  3. Set the target of the query. The target is the database table on which the query performs its action. Any of the three tables in the database can be the target of your query.

  4. Set the query type. The type can be any of the following.

    Query Type Attribute Sensitivity Description
    Search Keeps return values in sync with the custom and global attributes of the query's table. Read-only. Retrieves information from the specified attributes. Results selection is based upon the input parameters, filters, sort operators, and result specifiers.
    Find IDs Keeps return values in sync with the custom and global attributes of the query's table. Read-only. Retrieves selected information about the specified entity (row) in the table.
    Update ID Keeps parameters in sync with the custom and global attributes or the target table. Write-only. Updates the properties of the specified entity (row) in the table.
    Topology Keeps return values in sync with the custom and global attributes of the query's table. Read-only. Searches the table for competition topology information.
  5. Optionally, make the query attribute sensitive by putting a check in the check box. It is usually a good idea to make queries attribute sensitive. If the query is attribute sensitive, any changes to the attributes associated with the query cause the query to be updated to accommodate the changes.

Customizing Existing Queries

Every query has specific properties you can associate with it. The properties, which are shown as tabbed pages in the query's Properties window, fall into five categories.

  • General
  • Parameters
  • Filters
  • Sort Operators
  • Return Values

General

The General properties tab contains two properties that you can customize. The first sets the maximum results per page of data that the query retrieves. The second enables or disables attribute sensitivity.

Parameters

Parameters are passed as part of the query to the server. They provide the run-time information that the query needs to complete. For example, the Available Competitions query (which is a default query), contains the current time. The query uses this information because, of course, the player wants to see the competitions that are available at that specific time.

To add a parameter, use these steps.

  1. Click the query to which the parameter will be added.
  2. Click the Parameters tab in the Properties window.
  3. Click the Add button. A dialog box appears.
  4. If you want to use an attribute as a template for the parameter, select Use this Attribute as a template. Otherwise, choose Create an unrelated parameter, and then enter the name and type of the parameter.

You can modify an existing parameter by clicking on its name and on the Edit button. In the dialog box that appears, set the parameter's name and type, as appropriate.

To delete a parameter, select it and then click the Delete button.

Filters

If you assign a filter to a query, the filter sifts out information from the results that you specify as unneeded. To add a filter to a query, do the following.

  1. In the project tree, click the query to which the filter will be added.
  2. In the Properties window, click the Filters tab.
  3. Click the Add button.
  4. In the dialog box that appears, set the value of the left side of the comparison. This can be any attribute or parameter that is already defined.
  5. Choose the comparison operator from the drop-down list.
  6. Set the value for the right side of the comparison. This can be any attribute or constant that is already defined.

To edit an existing filter, first select it. Next, click the Edit button. XLAST displays a dialog box that enables you to change the value of the left or right side of the comparison. It also enable you to choose a different comparison operator.

If you need to delete a filter, select it and click the Delete button.

The filters are applied in the order in which you define them, from top to bottom. Therefore, changing the position of the query in the list affects the results of the query. To improve efficiency, put the most restrictive filters first. Doing so means that the remaining filters need to operate on less information.

Sort Operators

Sort operators sort the results of the query. You can add multiple sort operators to the same query. If you do, they will be applied in the order that you specify them, from top to bottom. To add a sort operator to a query, use the following steps.

  1. In the project tree, select the query to which you will add the sort operator.
  2. In the Properties window, click the Sort Operators tab.
  3. Click the Add button.
  4. In the dialog box that appears, use the drop-down list to select what you want to sort on.
  5. Set the order as Ascending or Descending.

Edit a query by selecting it in the list, and then clicking Edit. XLAST displays a dialog box that enables you to choose a different attribute. You can also modify the sort order.

To delete a sort operator, select it and click the Delete button.

Move the sort operator up or down in the list by selecting it and then clicking the Move Up or Move Down button.

Return Values

The return values of a query is the list of attributes whose values the query returns. Your query can return any or all of the attributes that are currently defined. Add a return value to the list by doing the following.

  1. In the project tree, select the query to which you will add the return value.
  2. In the Properties window, click the Return Values tab.
  3. Click the Add button.
  4. In the dialog box that appears, select the attribute you want to add to the list, and then click OK.

You can edit an existing return value by selecting it and then clicking Edit. XLAST displays a dialog box that lets you choose an attribute to replace the selected attribute.

Delete a return value by first selecting it from the list. Next, click the Delete button.

You can order the return values by using the Move Up and Move Down buttons.
